executiveSummary: The OmniCommerce Enterprise platform is currently in a high-fidelity prototype phase. While the frontend successfully demonstrates the intended UI/UX paradigms (using React, Framer Motion, and Tailwind CSS), it fundamentally falls short of the enterprise-grade architectural requirements and technical constraints. Critical deviations include the use of a standard React SPA instead of the mandated Next.js (SSR/SSG) framework, the absence of actual WebGL/Three.js implementations for the 3D hero section, and a backend that relies entirely on hardcoded mock data without database integration, authentication, or security middleware. The application currently lacks PCI-DSS compliance measures, real-time WebSocket integrations, and Elasticsearch capabilities. Immediate architectural refactoring is required to transition this prototype into a scalable, secure, and compliant production-ready system.

testCases.description: Verify that the recommendation grid updates asynchronously and supports infinite scrolling with WebP images.
testCases.notes: Grid loads mock data via fetch, but lacks infinite scrolling implementation. Images are served as standard JPEGs from Unsplash, not optimized WebP formats.

testCases.description: Verify that checkout calculates tax and shipping dynamically via API based on postal code.
testCases.notes: Tax and shipping are hardcoded on the frontend (8% tax, $15 shipping). The backend '/checkout/intent' endpoint is not utilized for dynamic calculation based on the ZIP code.

testCases.description: Verify that the inventory dashboard updates data via WebSockets for real-time accuracy.
testCases.notes: Dashboard uses a standard HTTP GET request via React useEffect. No WebSocket connection (e.g., Socket.io) is implemented.

testCases.description: Verify that the semantic search handles typographical errors and natural language queries.
testCases.notes: Search relies on a basic Array.prototype.filter() with String.includes(). It does not support typo tolerance, synonyms, or natural language processing. Missing Elasticsearch backend.

testCases.description: Verify that the B2B portal parses uploaded CSV files containing up to 10,000 rows.
testCases.notes: The 'Upload CSV' button is purely presentational. No file parsing logic (e.g., PapaParse) or backend bulk ingestion logic is implemented.

testCases.description: Verify that the system enforces Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) and JWT validation for administrative logins.
testCases.notes: No authentication middleware exists on the backend. The frontend hardcodes an admin user state. No SSO, MFA, or JWT logic is present.

codeAudit: The codebase exhibits severe architectural and security deficiencies relative to the project requirements. ARCHITECTURE: The frontend is built as a monolithic React SPA within a single 'App.tsx' file (over 600 lines), violating the Next.js SSR/SSG constraint required for SEO and performance. State management via Zustand is functional but mixes UI state with business logic. The backend is a rudimentary Express server lacking a layered architecture (Controllers, Services, Repositories). SECURITY: The backend lacks any form of input validation or sanitization, leaving it vulnerable to NoSQL/SQL injection if a real database were connected. The CORS policy is overly permissive ('app.use(cors())' without origin restrictions). There is no authentication, authorization, or rate-limiting middleware. PERFORMANCE: The frontend search implementation lacks debouncing, causing an API call on every keystroke after 2 characters. Large unoptimized images are loaded directly, which will negatively impact the required 95+ Lighthouse score. ACCESSIBILITY: The application fails WCAG 2.1 AA compliance due to missing ARIA labels, insufficient keyboard navigation support in custom dropdowns, and poor focus management.
identifiedIssues: Violation of Technical Constraints: Frontend is a standard React SPA, not Next.js (missing SSR/SSG).
Security Vulnerability: Overly permissive CORS configuration in backend/server.js.
Security Vulnerability: Complete lack of authentication, JWT validation, and RBAC enforcement on backend routes.
Security Vulnerability: Missing input sanitization and validation on all POST/GET endpoints (e.g., req.query.q, req.body).
Performance Issue: Missing debounce logic on the semantic search input, leading to potential API spam.
Architecture Flaw: Monolithic App.tsx file severely impacts maintainability and code readability.
Missing Feature: 3D Hero section uses a 2D image with drag constraints instead of actual WebGL/Three.js rendering.
Missing Feature: Checkout tax and shipping are hardcoded on the frontend instead of dynamically calculated via backend API.
Missing Feature: Inventory dashboard lacks WebSocket integration for real-time updates.
Accessibility Violation: Missing aria-labels, role attributes, and keyboard navigation support across interactive elements (Search dropdown, Custom Modals).
